oneOf.description: The accounts scan group, which includes operations related to scanning accounts.
The accounts data group, which includes operations related to fetching account data.
The admin group, which includes administrative operations.
The bank data group, which includes operations related to fetching bank data.
The full group, which includes all other operations.
The minimal group, which includes a minimal set of operations.
The surfnet cheatcodes group, which includes operations for testing and simulation.

data.description: The new account data, as a hex encoded string. This contains the actual data stored in the account.

executable.description: Whether the account should be executable (true for program accounts, false for data accounts).

lamports.description: The new balance in lamports (1 SOL = 1,000,000,000 lamports).

owner.description: The new owner program ID, as a base-58 encoded string.

rentEpoch.description: The new rent epoch in which this account will next owe rent.

keypairFile.description: Path to the keypair file for the authorized voter.

keypair.description: Byte array representing the keypair for the authorized voter.

sourceProgramId.description: The public key of the source program to clone, as a base-58 encoded string.

destinationProgramId.description: The public key of the destination program, as a base-58 encoded string.

commitment.description: The commitment describes how finalized a block is at that point in time. Options are 'processed', 'confirmed', or 'finalized'

CommitmentLevel.description:
An attribute of a slot. It describes how finalized a block is at some point in time. For example, a slot
is said to be at the max level immediately after the cluster recognizes the block at that slot as
finalized. When querying the ledger state, use lower levels of commitment to report progress and higher
levels to ensure state changes will not be rolled back.

oneOf.description:
The highest slot of the heaviest fork processed by the node. Ledger state at this slot is
not derived from a confirmed or finalized block, but if multiple forks are present, is from
the fork the validator believes is most likely to finalize.

The highest slot that has been voted on by supermajority of the cluster, ie. is confirmed.
Confirmation incorporates votes from gossip and replay. It does not count votes on
descendants of a block, only direct votes on that block, and upholds "optimistic
confirmation" guarantees in release 1.3 and onwards.

The highest slot having reached max vote lockout, as recognized by a supermajority of the
cluster.

accounts.description: The IDL accounts array defining all account types that the program can create or interact with.

includeParsedAccounts.description: If true, includes parsed account data in the snapshot.

filter.description: Filter configuration to limit which accounts are included.

scope.description: Scope to limit the accounts included. Options are 'network' (all accounts) or 'preTransaction' (accounts touched by a transaction).

CheatcodeFilter.description: Either the string literal "all" to target every cheatcode, or an explicit list of cheatcode method names (e.g. ["surfnet_setAccount", "surfnet_timeTravel"]).

oneOf.description: Target every registered cheatcode.
Explicit list of cheatcode method names (full `surfnet_*` form).

lockout.description: If true, the disable operation is permitted to disable `surfnet_enableCheatcode` and `surfnet_disableCheatcode` themselves, locking the cheatcode surface for the rest of the session.

cheatcodesFilter.description: Which cheatcodes to enable: either the string "all" or an explicit list of method names.
Which cheatcodes to disable: either the string "all" or an explicit list of method names.

config.description: Optional control config. When `lockout` is true, `surfnet_enableCheatcode` and `surfnet_disableCheatcode` may themselves be disabled.

requireTower.description: Boolean indicating if a tower is required for this identity.

identityKeypair.description: Byte array representing the identity keypair.

requireTower.description: Boolean indicating if a tower is required for this identity.

programId.description: The public key of the program, as a base-58 encoded string. This is the program whose upgrade authority will be modified.

newAuthority.description: The public key of the new authority, as a base-58 encoded string. If omitted, the program will have no upgrade authority (immutable).

publicTpuAddr.description: The public TPU address as a string.

publicTpuForwardsAddr.description: The public TPU forwards address as a string.

whitelist.description: A list of public keys (base-58 encoded strings) to set as the repair whitelist.

oneOf.description: Specifies an account, as a base-58 encoded string. Use this to set a specific account as the delegate or authority.
Specifies no account. Use this to remove a delegate or authority (set to None).

path.description: Path to the file containing staked nodes overrides.

update.description: The supply data to update. Contains new values for total, circulating, and non-circulating SOL amounts.

owner.description: The public key of the token account owner, as a base-58 encoded string. This is the wallet that owns the token account.

mint.description: The public key of the token mint, as a base-58 encoded string. This identifies the specific token type (e.g., USDC, SOL).

update.description: The token account data to update. Contains new values for balance, delegate, state, and authorities.

tokenProgram.description: The token program ID, as a base-58 encoded string. Defaults to SPL Token program if not specified.

transaction.description: The transaction to simulate, as a base-64 encoded string.

circulating.description: The new circulating supply of SOL in lamports (total supply minus non-circulating).

nonCirculating.description: The new non-circulating supply of SOL in lamports (locked in non-circulating accounts).

nonCirculatingAccounts.description: The new list of non-circulating account addresses that hold locked SOL.

total.description: The new total supply of SOL in lamports across the entire network.

config.description: Configuration specifying how to modify the clock. Can move to a specific epoch, slot, or timestamp.

oneOf.description: Move to the specified absolute epoch number (each epoch = 432,000 slots).
Move to the specified absolute slot number in the blockchain.
Move to the specified UNIX timestamp in milliseconds since epoch.

amount.description: The new token balance amount in the smallest unit (e.g., lamports for SOL, or the smallest token unit).

closeAuthority.description: The new close authority that can close the account and recover rent.

delegate.description: The new delegate account that can spend tokens on behalf of the owner.

delegatedAmount.description: The new delegated amount that the delegate is authorized to spend.

Sets or updates an account's properties including lamports, data, owner, and executable status.
Sets or updates a token account's properties including balance, delegate, state, and authorities.
Clones a program account from one program ID to another, including its associated program data.
Profiles a transaction to analyze compute units, account changes, and execution details.
Retrieves all profiling results for transactions tagged with a specific identifier.
Configures the network supply information including total, circulating, and non-circulating amounts.
Sets or removes the upgrade authority for a program's ProgramData account.
Retrieves the detailed profile of a specific transaction by signature or UUID.
Registers an IDL (Interface Definition Language) for a program to enable account data parsing.
Retrieves the registered IDL for a specific program ID at a given slot.
Retrieves the most recent transaction signatures from the local network.
Sets the network's current epoch info to a future time based on the supplied epoch, slot, or time.
Pauses the local network's clock progression, halting block production.
Resumes the network's block production if paused.
Resets an account on the local network to its original state from the remote datasource.
Resets the entire network to its initial state.
Exports a snapshot of all accounts in the Surfnet SVM.
Registers an account for streaming, downloading it from the datasource every time rather than caching it in the SVM.
Registers multiple accounts for streaming from the datasource in a single batch call. See also `surfnet_streamAccount`.
Prevents an account (and optionally accounts it owns) from being downloaded from the remote RPC. See also `surfnet_streamAccount`.
Retrieves the list of accounts currently registered for streaming.
Retrieves Surfnet network information including runbook execution status.
Writes program data at a specified offset, allowing deployment of large programs by sending data in chunks.
Registers a scenario with account overrides for testing different network states.
Re-enables one or more previously disabled Surfpool cheatcode RPC methods for the current session. See also `surfnet_disableCheatcode`.

Disables one or more Surfpool cheatcode RPC methods for the current session. Without `lockout=true`, `surfnet_enableCheatcode` and `surfnet_disableCheatcode` themselves cannot be disabled. See also `surfnet_enableCheatcode`.
